<div class="x2mdx-ref-schema" id="schema-com-digitalasset-canton-protocol-v30-acscommitment">
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-schema-head">
    <h3>com.digitalasset.canton.protocol.v30.AcsCommitment</h3>
    
    <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-summary">6 fields</p>
    
  </div>
  
  <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-description">A commitment to the active contract set (ACS) that is shared between two participants on a given synchronizer at a given time. Given a commitment scheme to the ACS, the semantics are as follows: the sender declares that the shared ACS was exactly the one committed to, at every commitment tick during the specified period and as determined by the period&#x27;s interval. The interval is assumed to be a round number of seconds. The ticks then start at the Java EPOCH time, and are exactly `interval` apart. All fields are mandatory.</p>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">

      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">string</span>
      
    </div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">

    </div>

<div class="x2mdx-ref-schema" id="schema-com-digitalasset-canton-crypto-v30-signaturedelegation">
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-schema-head">

    <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-summary">7 fields</p>
    
  </div>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">session_key</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">bytes</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">ASN.1 + DER-encoding of X.509 SubjectPublicKeyInfo structure of the session public key: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5280#section-4.1. This key was used to produce the actual signature.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">session_key_spec</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">SigningKeySpec</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">The signing key spec for the session key.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">validity_period_from_inclusive</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">int64</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">A timestamp in microseconds of UTC time since Unix epoch that indicates when the session key is considered valid.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">validity_period_duration_seconds</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">uint32</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Duration in seconds indicating how long the session key remains valid. The validity period starts from `validity_period_from_inclusive` above.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">format</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">SignatureFormat</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">The format of the signature</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">signature</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">bytes</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">The signature of the combined hash of the session key fingerprint, validity period, and synchronizer id, by the long-term key. This signature authorizes the session key to act on behalf of the long-term key.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">signing_algorithm_spec</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">SigningAlgorithmSpec</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">The signing algorithm specification used to produce this signature</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Recipients of the request. Must be non-empty. A recipient is either a member (cf. MemberRecipient) or a set of members (cf. GroupRecipient such as AllMembersOfSynchronizer). TARGET STATE: Each recipient must be authorized at the underlying synchronizer (cf. Recipient.isAuthorized). CURRENT STATE: A recipient must be &quot;known&quot; to the sequencer. A recipient in general remains &quot;known&quot; even after its topology transactions have been removed on the synchronizer.</div>

<div class="x2mdx-ref-schema" id="schema-com-digitalasset-canton-protocol-v30-decentralizednamespacedefinition">
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-schema-head">
    <h3>com.digitalasset.canton.protocol.v30.DecentralizedNamespaceDefinition</h3>
    
    <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-summary">3 fields</p>
    
  </div>
  
  <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-description">a decentralized namespace definition that creates a new namespace supported by the the original owners authorization: the decentralized namespace definition with serial = 1 must be authorized by all the owners of the namespace that form the decentralized namespace. for definitions with serial &gt; 1, we need the authorization of #threshold owners plus all new owners any further transaction within the decentralized namespace other than decentralized namespace definitions needs #threshold signatures of the owners UNIQUE(decentralized_namespace)</p>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">decentralized_namespace</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">string</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">name of the decentralized namespace, computed from the hash of its initial owners</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">threshold</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">int32</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">the threshold required for any subsequent update signing</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">owners</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">repeated string</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">the namespaces of the owners</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

    <li><code>ONBOARDING_RESTRICTION_RESTRICTED_OPEN</code></li>
    
    <li><code>ONBOARDING_RESTRICTION_RESTRICTED_LOCKED</code></li>
    
  </ul>
  
  
</div>

<div class="x2mdx-ref-schema" id="schema-com-digitalasset-canton-protocol-v30-trafficcontrolparameters">
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-schema-head">
    <h3>com.digitalasset.canton.protocol.v30.TrafficControlParameters</h3>
    
    <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-summary">7 fields</p>
    
  </div>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">max_base_traffic_amount</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">uint64</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">[doc-entry-start: TrafficControlParameters] In bytes, the maximum amount of base traffic that can be accumulated</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">max_base_traffic_accumulation_duration</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">Duration</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Maximum duration over which the base rate can be accumulated Consequently, base_traffic_rate = max_base_traffic_amount / max_base_traffic_accumulation_duration</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">read_vs_write_scaling_factor</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">uint32</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Read scaling factor to compute the event cost. In parts per 10 000.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">set_balance_request_submission_window_size</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">Duration</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Window size used to compute the max sequencing time of a submission request This impacts how quickly a submission is expected to be accepted before a retry should be attempted by the caller Default is 5 minutes</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">enforce_rate_limiting</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">bool</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">If true, submission requests without enough traffic credit will not be delivered</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">base_event_cost</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">uint64</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">In bytes, base event cost added to all sequenced events. Optional</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">free_confirmation_responses</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">bool</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Whether to charge for confirmation responses Default: false</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">repeated Signature</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Signatures on the combined hash computed over the transaction_hashes MUST contain at least one</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

<div class="x2mdx-ref-schema" id="schema-com-digitalasset-canton-protocol-v30-namespacedelegation">
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-schema-head">
    <h3>com.digitalasset.canton.protocol.v30.NamespaceDelegation</h3>
    
    <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-summary">6 fields</p>
    
  </div>
  
  <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-description">[start NamespaceDelegation definition] namespace delegation (equivalent to X509v3 CA root or intermediate CAs) if is_root_delegation==false, the target key may sign all mappings requiring a signature for the namespace except other NamespaceDelegation mappings. authorization: a namespace delegation is either signed by the root key, or is signed by a key for which there exists a series of properly authorized namespace delegations that are ultimately signed by the root key revocation: a revoked namespace delegation cannot be re-created. While the delegation itself is revoked, valid transactions that have been signed using the authority of the delegation before its revocation stay valid. UNIQUE(namespace, target_key)</p>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">

    <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-summary">3 fields</p>
    
  </div>
  
  <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-description">[doc-entry-start: PartyToKeyMapping] mapping a party to a key authorization: whoever controls the party uid UNIQUE(party)</p>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">party</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">string</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">the party</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">threshold</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">uint32</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">the authorization threshold</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">signing_keys</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">repeated SigningPublicKey</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">the designated signing keys</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

<div class="x2mdx-ref-schema" id="schema-com-digitalasset-canton-protocol-v30-partytoparticipant">
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-schema-head">
    <h3>com.digitalasset.canton.protocol.v30.PartyToParticipant</h3>
    
    <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-summary">4 fields</p>
    
  </div>
  
  <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-description">Mapping that maps a party to a participant The PartyToParticipant mapping may also specify a list of signing keys for setting up an external party, in which case the keys and the threshold take precedence over any PartyToKeyMapping for the same party. Additionally, the list of signing keys may contain the public key of the party&#x27;s namespace, which allows this mapping to authorize itself without the need of a NamespaceDelegation root certificate (called self-signed). authorization: the required authorization of the mapping is a union of the authorization for individual changes - threshold change: party namespace - adding a signing key: party namespace + all the new signing key - removing a signing key: party namespace - changing the signing key threshold: party namespace - upgrading a participant permission or adding a new participant: namespaces from party and the participant namespace - downgrading a participant permission or removing a participant: party namespace OR the participant namespace - setting a participant&#x27;s onboarding flag from false to true: party namespace - setting a participant&#x27;s onboarding flag from true to false: participant namespace - the removal of a PTP must be authorized just by the party revocation: Revoking a self-signed PTP does not prevent later re-creation of a PTP with the same partyId. To prevent further usage of the key associated with the party&#x27;s namespace, revoke a NamespaceDelegation root certificate for that namespace. UNIQUE(party)</p>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">party</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">string</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">the party that is to be represented by the participants</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">threshold</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">uint32</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">the signatory threshold required by the participants to be able to act on behalf of the party. a mapping with threshold &gt; 1 is considered a definition of a consortium party</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">participants</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">repeated HostingParticipant</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">which participants will host the party. if threshold &gt; 1, must be Confirmation or Observation. if all participants have Observation permission, the confirmation treshold is ignored, making the party a purely observing party.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">party_signing_keys</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">SigningKeysWithThreshold</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Contains protocol signing keys for the party used to authorize externally signed Daml transactions, along with a signing threshold. The max number of keys is 20</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

<div class="x2mdx-ref-schema" id="schema-com-digitalasset-canton-protocol-v30-partytoparticipant-hostingparticipant">

      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">Onboarding</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">optional, present iff the party is being onboarded to the participant</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">previous_timestamp</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">int64</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">The timestamp of the previous event of the member&#x27;s event sequence. in microseconds of UTC time since Unix epoch Optional, set to None if this is the first ever event of the member&#x27;s event sequence.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">timestamp</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">int64</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">The timestamp of the event. Unique and monotonically increasing. The same event has the same timestamp for all recipients. in microseconds of UTC time since Unix epoch</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">physical_synchronizer_id</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">string</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">The synchronizer on which the event has been sequenced.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">message_id</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">string</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">The message_id of the request. Optional, but will be defined iff the request sender is the subscriber.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">batch</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">CompressedBatch</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">The payload of the event. Optional, defined iff deliver_error_reason is undefined.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">

      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">Status</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Optional error, indicates why the underlying SubmissionRequest has been rejected.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">topology_timestamp</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">int64</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Optional timestamp of the topology snapshot used for processing the underlying request (e.g., validation, signing, resolving group addresses). If undefined, timestamp is used instead.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">traffic_receipt</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">TrafficReceipt</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Traffic receipt with information about the traffic state after the cost for this event has been consumed Optional - only set on deliver receipt sent back to the sender of the event when traffic control is enabled</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

<div class="x2mdx-ref-schema" id="schema-com-digitalasset-canton-protocol-v30-sequencersynchronizerstate">
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-schema-head">
    <h3>com.digitalasset.canton.protocol.v30.SequencerSynchronizerState</h3>
    
    <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-summary">4 fields</p>
    
  </div>
  
  <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-description">which sequencers are active on the given synchronizer authorization: whoever controls the synchronizer UNIQUE(synchronizer_id)</p>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">synchronizer_id</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">string</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">the synchronizer id of the sequencer group</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">threshold</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">uint32</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">The system can tolerate up to min(threshold - 1, (activeWithSigningKey.size - 1)/3) malicious active sequencers, where activeWithSigningKey is the set of sequencers from active that have an active OwnerToKeyMapping with a signing key. In order to make the system resilient against f malicious active sequencers, you need to: (1) configure threshold := f + 1, (2) possibly add active sequencers such that f &lt;= (activeWithSigningKey.size - 1)/3. (3) possibly register signing keys for active sequencers,</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">active</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">repeated string</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">the list of active sequencers If a sequencer without an active signing key is added to this set, it has no effect on the topology state.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">observers</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">repeated string</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">the list of observing sequencers (uid) within the group (read messages but don&#x27;t respond)</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

  </div>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">

    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">transaction</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">bytes</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">serialized topology transaction (protobuf bytestring)</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">signatures</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">repeated Signature</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">multiple signatures Either this field OR the multi_transaction_signatures field MUST contain at least one signature</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">proposal</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">bool</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">if true, this transaction is just a proposal. this means that every signature is valid, but the signatures are insufficient to properly authorize the transaction. proposals are distributed via the topology channel too. proposals will be pruned automatically when the nodes are pruned TODO(#14045) implement pruning</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">multi_transaction_signatures</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">repeated MultiTransactionSignatures</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">If set, the transaction may be authorized by signing a hash computed from multiple transaction hashes This allows to effectively authorize multiple transactions with a single signature. Each item MUST contain the hash of this transaction Optional</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

<div class="x2mdx-ref-schema" id="schema-com-digitalasset-canton-protocol-v30-submissionrequest">
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-schema-head">
    <h3>com.digitalasset.canton.protocol.v30.SubmissionRequest</h3>
    
    <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-summary">7 fields</p>
    
  </div>
  
  <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-description">Messages used for synchronization between sequencer nodes</p>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">sender</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">string</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Sender of the request. This request must be wrapped in a SignedContent and the signature therein must validate under one of the sender&#x27;s public keys. The request metadata must contain a valid authentication token belonging to the sender. (Details in SequencerAuthenticationServerInterceptor.) TARGET STATE: The sender must be authorized (cf. TopologySnapshot.isMemberKnown) CURRENT STATE: The sender authorization check might have a few gaps (e.g., see MemberAuthenticationService.isActive) and/or may use the wrong snapshot.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">message_id</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">string</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Will be included in the receipt of the request. The sender should not reuse a message_id until max_sequencing_time, because otherwise it will be unable to correlate receipts with requests.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">batch</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">CompressedBatch</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">The payload, mandatory At most one recipient of an envelope may be (a) a mediator, (b) a mediator group, or (c) all synchronizer members.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">max_sequencing_time</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">int64</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Maximum sequencing time for the request. If the request can&#x27;t be sequenced until the specified time, the sequencer will reject it. After max_sequencing_time, no event corresponding to the request will be emitted. in microseconds of UTC time since Unix epoch</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">topology_timestamp</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">int64</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Optional timestamp (in microseconds of UTC time since Unix epoch) of topology snapshot to be used for processing the request (e.g., validation, signing, resolving group addresses). If defined, it must meet the following conditions: (1) it must be before or at the sequencing timestamp, (2) it must be by at most DynamicSynchronizerParameters.sequencerTopologyTimestampTolerance before the sequencing timestamp, where the DynamicSynchronizerParameters are taken from the topology state at topology_timestamp, (3) the sequencer must have signing keys registered in the topology snapshot. If defined and invalid, the request is rejected. If undefined or invalid, the topology snapshot at sequencing time will be used instead.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">aggregation_rule</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">AggregationRule</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Optional rule used to aggregate requests.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">submission_cost</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">SequencingSubmissionCost</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Optional submission cost computed by the sender. Must be provided if traffic control is enabled on the receiving synchronizer.</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>
